top_signals.title: Bernstein Sets Long-Term Oil Price Target At $75 Per Barrel
top_signals.summary: Wall Street analyst Bernstein Research has established $75 per barrel as a reasonable long-term oil price target for equity valuations, pointing to rising marginal costs and declining reserves. Citing a new survey of the 50 largest energy companies globally, Bernstein analyst Neil Beveridge has revealed that higher spot prices and a tightening physical market continue to feed inflation back into the supply chain. This will negatively impact production costs, with the global marginal cost of oil now projected to climb to $77 per barrel, after declining…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: SpaceX IPO boldly steps into the unknown of AI economics

top_signals.title: Hyperliquid's pre-IPO SpaceX contracts suffers 45% flash crash, liquidating $1.5 million
Hyperliquid-based prop trading platform Hypernova raises $3 million in pre-seed funding
top_signals.summary: Hyperliquid-based prop trading platform Hypernova says it offers "instant payouts" and plans to publicly launch within the next two months.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
